title: helianthemum marifolium subsp. origanifolium
intro: Helianthemum marifolium subsp. origanifolium, also known as Rockrose or Origanoid Rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Cistus family, characterized by its fragrant, pinkish-white flowers and needle-like leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Helianthemum marifolium subsp. origanifolium are believed to provide various health benefits, including wound healing, antimicrobial activity, and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, have been used to treat skin issues and promote overall well-being.
